official_functions.role: Name the packet id assigned in Play clientbound state
Construct, write, decode, and observe the container id body
Write and dispatch-decode the full official Play clientbound container_close frame

invariants: The official Play clientbound table assigns container_close immediately after commands.
The official Play clientbound protocol codec dispatches the container_close id to ClientboundContainerClosePacket.
The official container_close body is one containerId value encoded through ClientboundContainerClosePacket.write(FriendlyByteBuf) and FriendlyByteBuf.writeContainerId.
The fixture uses the official ClientboundContainerClosePacket(int) constructor with a small non-zero container id, so it does not require an initialized Menu, screen, Level, or game state.

The framed bytes, packet table id, body bytes, decoded packet class, decoded container id, Play clientbound packet table, and remaining decode byte count are generated by the official jar-backed answer artifact.
This contract does not prove menu lifecycle behavior, screen close behavior, inventory state, initialized game state, runtime Play entry, world load, render readiness, or client-load completion.
test_topology_note: rust_test_target names the current case adapter only; the manifest owns the runnable test topology.
stop_boundary: Do not use this framed dispatch/decode contract to infer menu lifecycle behavior, screen close behavior, inventory state, initialized game state, runtime Play entry, world load, render readiness, or client-load completion.
